title: HTML Scripting Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential tags and attributes for adding JavaScript into your HTML documents.

questions.question: Which tag is used to embed or reference executable code, primarily JavaScript, in an HTML file?
What happens when the browser encounters a standard <script> tag in the HTML document?
options: It downloads the script and continues parsing the HTML in parallel.
It ignores the script until the page is fully loaded.
It pauses the parsing of the HTML until the script is downloaded and executed.
It automatically redirects the user to the script's source URL.

questions.question: Which attribute should you use if you want a script to execute ONLY after the entire HTML document has been parsed?
The <noscript> tag is used for what purpose?
options: To provide alternate content for users with JavaScript disabled.
To prevent a script from being executed by specific browsers.
To define a primary heading for a script section.
To compress the size of a JavaScript file.

questions.question: Where is the recommended position for script tags that don't use 'defer' or 'async' to ensure fast page loading?
options: At the very beginning of the <body> tag.
